title: Claudin1 IHC Morphology of Human Vagus Nerve
author: Nicole A. Pelot
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6fzhbp6

steps.title: We used Nikon's NIS Elements software (v5
steps.instruction: We used Nikon's NIS Elements software (v5.02.01, Build 1270) to segment human vagus nerve immunohistochemical micrographs (anti-claudin-1) using the General Analysis RGB tool.

steps.title: We converted the binary segmented image...
steps.instruction: We converted the binary segmented image into “Graticule Masks”, binary images saved as TIFs.

steps.title: We imported the TIFs into Matlab...
steps.instruction: We imported the TIFs into Matlab and generated a data structure of the x and y coordinates of the pixels for each closed boundary of the loaded binary images using the bwboundaries function.

steps.title: We scaled the pixel coordinates to...
steps.instruction: We scaled the pixel coordinates to microns using the segmented scale bar.

steps.title: For each image, we selected preprocessing...
steps.instruction: For each image, we selected preprocessing steps, such as smoothing and sharpening.

steps.title: For each image, we selected ranges...
steps.instruction: For each image, we selected ranges of hues, saturations, and intensities to values that identify the perineurium and different values to identify the entirety of the nerve.

steps.title: For each image, we selected postprocessing...
steps.instruction: For each image, we selected postprocessing steps, such as setting a minimum size criterion (eliminate small off-target regions), smoothing, cleaning, closing, and filling holes.

steps.title: We made manual adjustments as needed,...
steps.instruction: We made manual adjustments as needed, including manual deletion of off-target regions and filling of target areas that had not been captured.

steps.title: We calculated cross-sectional area of each...
steps.instruction: We calculated cross-sectional area of each fascicle (inner perineurium and outer perineurium traces) and nerve using Matlab’s polyarea. Effective diameter (for a nerve or fascicle) is the diameter of the circle that has the same cross-sectional area as the raw trace. The perineurium thickness is half of the difference in effective diameters of the inner and outer perineurium traces.

steps.title: Each sample was imaged at 10x...
steps.instruction: Each sample was imaged at 10x using a Nikon Ti2 microscope with a Photometrics Prime 95B-25MM camera (Nikon Instruments Inc.). We selected the best of four slices for each sample based on the quality of the slice (no tearing or fraying).

steps.title: We stored the pixel coordinates with...
steps.instruction: We stored the pixel coordinates with indexing that assigns a fascicle number, which were then checked so that both the interior and exterior perineurium trace relate to the same fascicle.

steps.title: Bake slides with sections of paraffin-embedded...
steps.instruction: Bake slides with sections of paraffin-embedded vagus nerve at overnight at 60oC and then cool overnight.

steps.title: Deparaffinize the slides and hydrate them to distilled water
steps.instruction: Deparaffinize the slides and hydrate them to distilled water: xylene (2x 6 min), 100% ethanol (5 min), 95% ethanol (4 min), 70% ethanol (3 min), deionized water (2x 1 min).

steps.title: Perform heat-induced epitope retrieval (HIER) at...
steps.instruction: Perform heat-induced epitope retrieval (HIER) at 120oC for 30 s followed by 90oC for 10 s, using a buffer with pH 6.0 (Thermo, TA-135-HBL).

steps.title: Cool for 20 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool for 20 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Rinse in deionized water (2x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in deionized water (2x 2 min).

steps.title: Block with 3% H2O2 diluted in...
steps.instruction: Block with 3% H2O2 diluted in deionized water for 10 min.

steps.title: Rinse in deionized water (2x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in deionized water (2x 2 min).

steps.title: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min).

steps.title: Block using DAKO Protein Block (X0909)...
steps.instruction: Block using DAKO Protein Block (X0909) for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Apply the primary antibody (rabbit anti-claudin-1,...
steps.instruction: Apply the primary antibody (rabbit anti-claudin-1, Abcam, ab15098) diluted in Thermo Antibody Diluent to a concentration of 1:50, and incubate overnight at 4oC.

steps.title: Rinse in Tris Tween buffer (2x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris Tween buffer (2x 2 min).

steps.title: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min).

steps.title: Apply the secondary antibody (biotinylated SP-conjugated...
steps.instruction: Apply the secondary antibody (biotinylated SP-conjugated Affinipure goat anti-rabbit IgG (H+L), Jackson, 111-065-144) diluted in Thermo Antibody Diluent to a concentration of 1:500, and incubate for 1 hour at room temperature.

steps.title: Rinse in Tris Tween buffer (2x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris Tween buffer (2x 2 min).

steps.title: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min).

steps.title: Apply ABC Elite (Vector, PK-6100) at...
steps.instruction: Apply ABC Elite (Vector, PK-6100) at a concentration of 1:50 for 30 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Rinse in Tris Tween buffer (2x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris Tween buffer (2x 2 min).

steps.title: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in Tris buffer (1x 2 min).

steps.title: Apply DAB chromogen (Thermo, TA-125-QHDX) for...
steps.instruction: Apply DAB chromogen (Thermo, TA-125-QHDX) for 1 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Rinse in deionized water (2x 2 min)
steps.instruction: Rinse in deionized water (2x 2 min).

steps.title: Counterstain using hematoxylin
steps.instruction: Counterstain using hematoxylin.

steps.title: Dehydrate, clear, and coverslip using DPX mountant
steps.instruction: Dehydrate, clear, and coverslip using DPX mountant.
